notes: Arturia Prophet VS V emulates the Sequential Circuits Prophet VS, the first commercial synthesizer to use vector synthesis — a technique where a joystick continuously crossfades between four independent digital oscillators arranged at the corners of a square. Each oscillator has its own waveform from a library of 128 digital waves, and the joystick position determines the mix of all four, allowing smooth morphing between starkly different timbres. The VS is famous for its ability to evolve and breathe in ways impossible with conventional subtractive synthesis, and Arturia's version adds an analog-modelled filter absent from the original. Vector X and Y position controls are the defining performance gestures, making the instrument ideal for animated pads, morphing textures, and evolving soundscapes.

Expression.description: Expression — scales output level for dynamics

Portamento Time.description: Portamento time — glide speed between pitches

Vector X Position.description: Vector joystick X position — crossfades between oscillator A+D (left) and B+C (right); the primary timbral morphing control

Vector Y Position.description: Vector joystick Y position — crossfades between oscillator A+B (top) and C+D (bottom); combined with X creates four-way morphing

Vector LFO Rate.description: Vector LFO rate — automated movement of the joystick position for continuously evolving morphs

Vector LFO Depth.description: Vector LFO depth — amount of automated joystick movement

Filter Cutoff.description: Low-pass filter cutoff — Arturia's analog-modelled filter addition to the VS architecture

Filter Resonance.description: Filter resonance — adds character and peak to the filter

Detune.description: Oscillator detune — spreads the four oscillators for chorusing and ensemble width

Reverb.description: Reverb send level — adds space and depth to evolving textures

Chorus.description: Chorus depth — further widens the vector synthesis stereo field
